name: omop-cdm
description: OMOP Common Data Model version 5.4 in LinkML

person.name: person
person.description: This table serves as the central identity management for all Persons in the database. It contains records that uniquely identify each person or patient, and some demographic information.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: year_of_birth
slots.description: NA

slots.name: month_of_birth
slots.description: NA

slots.name: day_of_birth
slots.description: NA

slots.name: birth_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: location_id
slots.description: The location_id from the LOCATION table representing the physical location of the care_site.

slots.name: provider_id
slots.description: It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.

slots.name: care_site_id
slots.description: This is the CARE_SITE_ID for the location that the provider primarily practices in.

slots.name: person_source_value
slots.description: Use this field to link back to persons in the source data. This is typically used for error checking of ETL logic.

slots.name: gender_source_value
slots.description: This is provider's gender as it appears in the source data.

slots.name: race_source_value
slots.description: This field is used to store the race of the person from the source data. It is not intended for use in standard analytics but for reference only.

slots.name: ethnicity_source_value
slots.description: This field is used to store the ethnicity of the person from the source data. It is not intended for use in standard analytics but for reference only.

observation_period.name: observation_period
observation_period.description: This table contains records which define spans of time during which two conditions are expected to hold: (i) Clinical Events that happened to the Person are recorded in the Event tables, and (ii) absence of records indicate such Events did not occur during this span of time.

slots.name: observation_period_id
slots.description: A Person can have multiple discrete Observation Periods which are identified by the Observation_Period_Id.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: observation_period_start_date
slots.description: Use this date to determine the start date of the Observation Period.

slots.name: observation_period_end_date
slots.description: Use this date to determine the end date of the period for which we can assume that all events for a Person are recorded.

visit_occurrence.name: visit_occurrence
visit_occurrence.description: This table contains Events where Persons engage with the healthcare system for a duration of time. They are often also called "Encounters". Visits are defined by a configuration of circumstances under which they occur, such as (i) whether the patient comes to a healthcare institution, the other way around, or the interaction is remote, (ii) whether and what kind of trained medical staff is delivering the service during the Visit, and (iii) whether the Visit is transient or for a longer period involving a stay in bed.

slots.name: visit_occurrence_id
slots.description: The Visit during which the note was written.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: visit_start_date
slots.description: For inpatient visits, the start date is typically the admission date. For outpatient visits the start date and end date will be the same.

slots.name: visit_start_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: visit_end_date
slots.description: For inpatient visits the end date is typically the discharge date.  If a Person is still an inpatient in the hospital at the time of the data extract and does not have a visit_end_date, then set the visit_end_date to the date of the data pull.

slots.name: visit_end_datetime
slots.description: If a Person is still an inpatient in the hospital at the time of the data extract and does not have a visit_end_datetime, then set the visit_end_datetime to the datetime of the data pull.

slots.name: provider_id
slots.description: It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.

slots.name: care_site_id
slots.description: This is the CARE_SITE_ID for the location that the provider primarily practices in.

slots.name: visit_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the kind of visit that took place (inpatient, outpatient, emergency, etc.)

slots.name: admitted_from_source_value
slots.description: NA

slots.name: discharged_to_source_value
slots.description: NA

slots.name: preceding_visit_occurrence_id
slots.description: Use this field to find the visit that occurred for the person prior to the given visit. There could be a few days or a few years in between.

visit_detail.name: visit_detail
visit_detail.description: The VISIT_DETAIL table is an optional table used to represents details of each record in the parent VISIT_OCCURRENCE table. A good example of this would be the movement between units in a hospital during an inpatient stay or claim lines associated with a one insurance claim. For every record in the VISIT_OCCURRENCE table there may be 0 or more records in the VISIT_DETAIL table with a 1:n relationship where n may be 0. The VISIT_DETAIL table is structurally very similar to VISIT_OCCURRENCE table and belongs to the visit domain.

slots.name: visit_detail_id
slots.description: The Visit Detail during which the note was written.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: visit_detail_start_date
slots.description: This is the date of the start of the encounter. This may or may not be equal to the date of the Visit the Visit Detail is associated with.

slots.name: visit_detail_start_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: visit_detail_end_date
slots.description: This the end date of the patient-provider interaction.  If a Person is still an inpatient in the hospital at the time of the data extract and does not have a visit_end_date, then set the visit_end_date to the date of the data pull.

slots.name: visit_detail_end_datetime
slots.description: If a Person is still an inpatient in the hospital at the time of the data extract and does not have a visit_end_datetime, then set the visit_end_datetime to the datetime of the data pull.

slots.name: provider_id
slots.description: It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.

slots.name: care_site_id
slots.description: This is the CARE_SITE_ID for the location that the provider primarily practices in.

slots.name: visit_detail_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the kind of visit detail that took place (inpatient, outpatient, emergency, etc.)

slots.name: admitted_from_source_value
slots.description: NA

slots.name: discharged_to_source_value
slots.description: NA

slots.name: preceding_visit_detail_id
slots.description: Use this field to find the visit detail that occurred for the person prior to the given visit detail record. There could be a few days or a few years in between.

slots.name: parent_visit_detail_id
slots.description: Use this field to find the visit detail that subsumes the given visit detail record. This is used in the case that a visit detail record needs to be nested beyond the VISIT_OCCURRENCE/VISIT_DETAIL relationship.

slots.name: visit_occurrence_id
slots.description: The Visit during which the note was written.

condition_occurrence.name: condition_occurrence
condition_occurrence.description: This table contains records of Events of a Person suggesting the presence of a disease or medical condition stated as a diagnosis, a sign, or a symptom, which is either observed by a Provider or reported by the patient.

slots.name: condition_occurrence_id
slots.description: The unique key given to a condition record for a person. Refer to the ETL for how duplicate conditions during the same visit were handled.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: condition_start_date
slots.description: Use this date to determine the start date of the condition

slots.name: condition_start_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: condition_end_date
slots.description: Use this date to determine the end date of the condition

slots.name: condition_end_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: stop_reason
slots.description: The reason a person stopped a medication as it is represented in the source. Reasons include regimen completed, changed, removed, etc. This field will be retired in v6.0.

slots.name: provider_id
slots.description: It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.

slots.name: visit_occurrence_id
slots.description: The Visit during which the note was written.

slots.name: visit_detail_id
slots.description: The Visit Detail during which the note was written.

slots.name: condition_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the condition that occurred. For example, this could be an ICD10 or Read code.

slots.name: condition_status_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the condition status.

drug_exposure.name: drug_exposure
drug_exposure.description: This table captures records about the exposure to a Drug ingested or otherwise introduced into the body. A Drug is a biochemical substance formulated in such a way that when administered to a Person it will exert a certain biochemical effect on the metabolism. Drugs include prescription and over-the-counter medicines, vaccines, and large-molecule biologic therapies. Radiological devices ingested or applied locally do not count as Drugs.

slots.name: drug_exposure_id
slots.description: The unique key given to records of drug dispensings or administrations for a person. Refer to the ETL for how duplicate drugs during the same visit were handled.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: drug_exposure_start_date
slots.description: Use this date to determine the start date of the drug record.

slots.name: drug_exposure_start_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: drug_exposure_end_date
slots.description: The DRUG_EXPOSURE_END_DATE denotes the day the drug exposure ended for the patient.

slots.name: drug_exposure_end_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: verbatim_end_date
slots.description: This is the end date of the drug exposure as it appears in the source data, if it is given

slots.name: stop_reason
slots.description: The reason a person stopped a medication as it is represented in the source. Reasons include regimen completed, changed, removed, etc. This field will be retired in v6.0.

slots.name: refills
slots.description: This is only filled in when the record is coming from a prescription written this field is meant to represent intended refills at time of the prescription.

slots.name: quantity
slots.description: The amount of specimen collected from the person.

slots.name: days_supply
slots.description: NA

slots.name: sig
slots.description: This is the verbatim instruction for the drug as written by the provider.

slots.name: lot_number
slots.description: NA

slots.name: provider_id
slots.description: It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.

slots.name: visit_occurrence_id
slots.description: The Visit during which the note was written.

slots.name: visit_detail_id
slots.description: The Visit Detail during which the note was written.

slots.name: drug_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the drug exposure that occurred. For example, this could be an NDC or Gemscript code.

slots.name: route_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the drug route.

slots.name: dose_unit_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the dose unit of the drug given.

procedure_occurrence.name: procedure_occurrence
procedure_occurrence.description: This table contains records of activities or processes ordered by, or carried out by, a healthcare provider on the patient with a diagnostic or therapeutic purpose.

slots.name: procedure_occurrence_id
slots.description: The unique key given to a procedure record for a person. Refer to the ETL for how duplicate procedures during the same visit were handled.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: procedure_date
slots.description: Use this date to determine the date the procedure started.

slots.name: procedure_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: procedure_end_date
slots.description: Use this field to house the date that the procedure ended.

slots.name: procedure_end_datetime
slots.description: Use this field to house the datetime that the procedure ended.

slots.name: quantity
slots.description: The amount of specimen collected from the person.

slots.name: provider_id
slots.description: It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.

slots.name: visit_occurrence_id
slots.description: The Visit during which the note was written.

slots.name: visit_detail_id
slots.description: The Visit Detail during which the note was written.

slots.name: procedure_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the procedure that occurred. For example, this could be an CPT4 or OPCS4 code.

slots.name: modifier_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the modifier code for the procedure that occurred.

device_exposure.name: device_exposure
device_exposure.description: The Device domain captures information about a person's exposure to a foreign physical object or instrument which is used for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes through a mechanism beyond chemical action. Devices include implantable objects (e.g. pacemakers, stents, artificial joints), medical equipment and supplies (e.g. bandages, crutches, syringes), other instruments used in medical procedures (e.g. sutures, defibrillators) and material used in clinical care (e.g. adhesives, body material, dental material, surgical material).

slots.name: device_exposure_id
slots.description: The unique key given to records a person's exposure to a foreign physical object or instrument.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: device_exposure_start_date
slots.description: Use this date to determine the start date of the device record.

slots.name: device_exposure_start_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: device_exposure_end_date
slots.description: The DEVICE_EXPOSURE_END_DATE denotes the day the device exposure ended for the patient, if given.

slots.name: device_exposure_end_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: unique_device_id
slots.description: This is the Unique Device Identification (UDI-DI) number for devices regulated by the FDA, if given.

slots.name: production_id
slots.description: This is the Production Identifier (UDI-PI) portion of the Unique Device Identification.

slots.name: quantity
slots.description: The amount of specimen collected from the person.

slots.name: provider_id
slots.description: It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.

slots.name: visit_occurrence_id
slots.description: The Visit during which the note was written.

slots.name: visit_detail_id
slots.description: The Visit Detail during which the note was written.

slots.name: device_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the device exposure that occurred. For example, this could be an NDC or Gemscript code.

slots.name: unit_source_value
slots.description: NA

measurement.name: measurement
measurement.description: The MEASUREMENT table contains records of Measurements, i.e. structured values (numerical or categorical) obtained through systematic and standardized examination or testing of a Person or Person's sample. The MEASUREMENT table contains both orders and results of such Measurements as laboratory tests, vital signs, quantitative findings from pathology reports, etc. Measurements are stored as attribute value pairs, with the attribute as the Measurement Concept and the value representing the result. The value can be a Concept (stored in VALUE_AS_CONCEPT), or a numerical value (VALUE_AS_NUMBER) with a Unit (UNIT_CONCEPT_ID). The Procedure for obtaining the sample is housed in the PROCEDURE_OCCURRENCE table, though it is unnecessary to create a PROCEDURE_OCCURRENCE record for each measurement if one does not exist in the source data. Measurements differ from Observations in that they require a standardized test or some other activity to generate a quantitative or qualitative result. If there is no result, it is assumed that the lab test was conducted but the result was not captured.

slots.name: measurement_id
slots.description: The unique key given to a Measurement record for a Person. Refer to the ETL for how duplicate Measurements during the same Visit were handled.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: measurement_date
slots.description: Use this date to determine the date of the measurement.

slots.name: measurement_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: measurement_time
slots.description: NA

slots.name: value_as_number
slots.description: This is the numerical value of the result of the Metadata, if applicable and available. It is not expected that all Metadata will have numeric results, rather, this field is here to house values should they exist.

slots.name: range_low
slots.description: Ranges have the same unit as the VALUE_AS_NUMBER. These ranges are provided by the source and should remain NULL if not given.

slots.name: range_high
slots.description: Ranges have the same unit as the VALUE_AS_NUMBER. These ranges are provided by the source and should remain NULL if not given.

slots.name: provider_id
slots.description: It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.

slots.name: visit_occurrence_id
slots.description: The Visit during which the note was written.

slots.name: visit_detail_id
slots.description: The Visit Detail during which the note was written.

slots.name: measurement_source_value
slots.description: This field contains the exact value from the source data that represents the measurement that occurred.

slots.name: unit_source_value
slots.description: NA

slots.name: value_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim result value of the Observation from the source data.  Do not get confused with the Observation_source_value which captures source value of the observation mapped to observation_concept_id. This field is the observation result value from the source.

slots.name: measurement_event_id
slots.description: If the Measurement record is related to another record in the database, this field is the primary key of the linked record.

observation.name: observation
observation.description: The OBSERVATION table captures clinical facts about a Person obtained in the context of examination, questioning or a procedure. Any data that cannot be represented by any other domains, such as social and lifestyle facts, medical history, family history, etc. are recorded here.

slots.name: observation_id
slots.description: The unique key given to an Observation record for a Person. Refer to the ETL for how duplicate Observations during the same Visit were handled.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: observation_date
slots.description: The date of when the Observation was obtained. Depending on what the Observation represents this could be the date of a lab test, the date of a survey, or the date a patient's family history was taken.

slots.name: observation_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: value_as_number
slots.description: This is the numerical value of the result of the Metadata, if applicable and available. It is not expected that all Metadata will have numeric results, rather, this field is here to house values should they exist.

slots.name: value_as_string
slots.description: NA

slots.name: provider_id
slots.description: It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.

slots.name: visit_occurrence_id
slots.description: The Visit during which the note was written.

slots.name: visit_detail_id
slots.description: The Visit Detail during which the note was written.

slots.name: observation_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the Observation that occurred. For example, this could be an ICD10 or Read code.

slots.name: unit_source_value
slots.description: NA

slots.name: qualifier_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing the qualifier of the Observation that occurred.

slots.name: value_source_value
slots.description: This field houses the verbatim result value of the Observation from the source data.  Do not get confused with the Observation_source_value which captures source value of the observation mapped to observation_concept_id. This field is the observation result value from the source.

slots.name: observation_event_id
slots.description: If the Observation record is related to another record in the database, this field is the primary key of the linked record.

death.name: death
death.description: The death domain contains the clinical event for how and when a Person dies. A person can have up to one record if the source system contains evidence about the Death, such as: Condition in an administrative claim, status of enrollment into a health plan, or explicit record in EHR data.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: death_date
slots.description: The date the person was deceased.

slots.name: death_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: cause_source_value
slots.description: NA

note.name: note
note.description: The NOTE table captures unstructured information that was recorded by a provider about a patient in free text (in ASCII, or preferably in UTF8 format) notes on a given date. The type of note_text is CLOB or varchar(MAX) depending on RDBMS.

slots.name: note_id
slots.description: This is the NOTE_ID for the NOTE record the NLP record is associated to.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: note_date
slots.description: The date the note was recorded.

slots.name: note_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: note_title
slots.description: The title of the note.

slots.name: note_text
slots.description: The content of the note.

slots.name: provider_id
slots.description: It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.

slots.name: visit_occurrence_id
slots.description: The Visit during which the note was written.

slots.name: visit_detail_id
slots.description: The Visit Detail during which the note was written.

slots.name: note_source_value
slots.description: NA

slots.name: note_event_id
slots.description: If the Note record is related to another record in the database, this field is the primary key of the linked record.

note_nlp.name: note_nlp
note_nlp.description: The NOTE_NLP table encodes all output of NLP on clinical notes. Each row represents a single extracted term from a note.

slots.name: note_nlp_id
slots.description: A unique identifier for the NLP record.

slots.name: note_id
slots.description: This is the NOTE_ID for the NOTE record the NLP record is associated to.

slots.name: snippet
slots.description: A small window of text surrounding the term

slots.name: offset
slots.description: Character offset of the extracted term in the input note

slots.name: lexical_variant
slots.description: Raw text extracted from the NLP tool.

slots.name: nlp_system
slots.description: NA

slots.name: nlp_date
slots.description: The date of the note processing.

slots.name: nlp_datetime
slots.description: The date and time of the note processing.

slots.name: term_exists
slots.description: NA

slots.name: term_temporal
slots.description: NA

slots.name: term_modifiers
slots.description: NA

specimen.name: specimen
specimen.description: The specimen domain contains the records identifying biological samples from a person.

slots.name: specimen_id
slots.description: Unique identifier for each specimen.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: specimen_date
slots.description: The date the specimen was collected.

slots.name: specimen_datetime
slots.description: NA

slots.name: quantity
slots.description: The amount of specimen collected from the person.

slots.name: specimen_source_id
slots.description: This is the identifier for the specimen from the source system.

slots.name: specimen_source_value
slots.description: NA

slots.name: unit_source_value
slots.description: NA

slots.name: anatomic_site_source_value
slots.description: NA

slots.name: disease_status_source_value
slots.description: NA

fact_relationship.name: fact_relationship
fact_relationship.description: The FACT_RELATIONSHIP table contains records about the relationships between facts stored as records in any table of the CDM. Relationships can be defined between facts from the same domain, or different domains. Examples of Fact Relationships include: [Person relationships](https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms?domain=Relationship&standardConcept=Standard&page=2&pageSize=15&query=) (parent-child), care site relationships (hierarchical organizational structure of facilities within a health system), indication relationship (between drug exposures and associated conditions), usage relationships (of devices during the course of an associated procedure), or facts derived from one another (measurements derived from an associated specimen).

slots.name: fact_id_1
slots.description: NA

slots.name: fact_id_2
slots.description: NA

location.name: location
location.description: The LOCATION table represents a generic way to capture physical location or address information of Persons and Care Sites.

slots.name: location_id
slots.description: The location_id from the LOCATION table representing the physical location of the care_site.

slots.name: address_1
slots.description: This is the first line of the address.

slots.name: address_2
slots.description: This is the second line of the address

slots.name: city
slots.description: NA

slots.name: state
slots.description: NA

slots.name: zip
slots.description: NA

slots.name: county
slots.description: NA

slots.name: location_source_value
slots.description: NA

slots.name: country_source_value
slots.description: The name of the country.

slots.name: latitude
slots.description: NA

slots.name: longitude
slots.description: NA

care_site.name: care_site
care_site.description: The CARE_SITE table contains a list of uniquely identified institutional (physical or organizational) units where healthcare delivery is practiced (offices, wards, hospitals, clinics, etc.).

slots.name: care_site_id
slots.description: This is the CARE_SITE_ID for the location that the provider primarily practices in.

slots.name: care_site_name
slots.description: The name of the care_site as it appears in the source data

slots.name: location_id
slots.description: The location_id from the LOCATION table representing the physical location of the care_site.

slots.name: care_site_source_value
slots.description: The identifier of the care_site as it appears in the source data. This could be an identifier separate from the name of the care_site.

slots.name: place_of_service_source_value
slots.description: NA

provider.name: provider
provider.description: The PROVIDER table contains a list of uniquely identified healthcare providers; duplication is not allowed. These are individuals providing hands-on healthcare to patients, such as physicians, nurses, midwives, physical therapists etc.

slots.name: provider_id
slots.description: It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.

slots.name: provider_name
slots.description: This field contains information that describes a healthcare provider.

slots.name: npi
slots.description: This is the National Provider Number issued to health care providers in the US by the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS).

slots.name: dea
slots.description: This is the identifier issued by the DEA, a US federal agency, that allows a provider to write prescriptions for controlled substances.

slots.name: care_site_id
slots.description: This is the CARE_SITE_ID for the location that the provider primarily practices in.

slots.name: year_of_birth
slots.description: NA

slots.name: provider_source_value
slots.description: Use this field to link back to providers in the source data. This is typically used for error checking of ETL logic.

slots.name: specialty_source_value
slots.description: This refers to the specific type of healthcare provider or field of expertise listed in the source data, encompassing physician specialties like internal medicine, emergency medicine, etc., as well as allied health professionals such as nurses, midwives, and pharmacists. It covers medical specialties like surgery, internal medicine, and radiology, while other services like prosthetics, acupuncture, and physical therapy fall under the domain of "Service."

slots.name: gender_source_value
slots.description: This is provider's gender as it appears in the source data.

payer_plan_period.name: payer_plan_period
payer_plan_period.description: The PAYER_PLAN_PERIOD table captures details of the period of time that a Person is continuously enrolled under a specific health Plan benefit structure from a given Payer. Each Person receiving healthcare is typically covered by a health benefit plan, which pays for (fully or partially), or directly provides, the care. These benefit plans are provided by payers, such as health insurances or state or government agencies. In each plan the details of the health benefits are defined for the Person or her family, and the health benefit Plan might change over time typically with increasing utilization (reaching certain cost thresholds such as deductibles), plan availability and purchasing choices of the Person. The unique combinations of Payer organizations, health benefit Plans and time periods in which they are valid for a Person are recorded in this table.

slots.name: payer_plan_period_id
slots.description: NA

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: payer_plan_period_start_date
slots.description: Start date of Plan coverage.

slots.name: payer_plan_period_end_date
slots.description: End date of Plan coverage.

slots.name: payer_source_value
slots.description: This is the Payer as it appears in the source data.

slots.name: plan_source_value
slots.description: This is the health benefit Plan of the Person as it appears in the source data.

slots.name: sponsor_source_value
slots.description: The Plan sponsor as it appears in the source data.

slots.name: family_source_value
slots.description: The common identifier for all people (often a family) that covered by the same policy.

slots.name: stop_reason_source_value
slots.description: The Plan stop reason as it appears in the source data.

cost.name: cost
cost.description:
The COST table captures records containing the cost of any medical event recorded in one of the OMOP clinical event tables such as DRUG_EXPOSURE, PROCEDURE_OCCURRENCE, VISIT_OCCURRENCE, VISIT_DETAIL, DEVICE_OCCURRENCE, OBSERVATION or MEASUREMENT.

Each record in the cost table account for the amount of money transacted for the clinical event. So, the COST table may be used to represent both receivables (charges) and payments (paid), each transaction type represented by its COST_CONCEPT_ID. The COST_TYPE_CONCEPT_ID field will use concepts in the Standardized Vocabularies to designate the source (provenance) of the cost data. A reference to the health plan information in the PAYER_PLAN_PERIOD table is stored in the record for information used for the adjudication system to determine the persons benefit for the clinical event.

slots.name: cost_id
slots.description: NA

slots.name: cost_event_id
slots.description: NA

slots.name: cost_domain_id
slots.description: NA

slots.name: total_charge
slots.description: NA

slots.name: total_cost
slots.description: NA

slots.name: total_paid
slots.description: NA

slots.name: paid_by_payer
slots.description: NA

slots.name: paid_by_patient
slots.description: NA

slots.name: paid_patient_copay
slots.description: NA

slots.name: paid_patient_coinsurance
slots.description: NA

slots.name: paid_patient_deductible
slots.description: NA

slots.name: paid_by_primary
slots.description: NA

slots.name: paid_ingredient_cost
slots.description: NA

slots.name: paid_dispensing_fee
slots.description: NA

slots.name: payer_plan_period_id
slots.description: NA

slots.name: amount_allowed
slots.description: NA

slots.name: revenue_code_source_value
slots.description: Revenue codes are a method to charge for a class of procedures and conditions in the U.S. hospital system.

slots.name: drg_source_value
slots.description: Diagnosis Related Groups are US codes used to classify hospital cases into one of approximately 500 groups.

drug_era.name: drug_era
drug_era.description: A Drug Era is defined as a span of time when the Person is assumed to be exposed to a particular active ingredient. A Drug Era is not the same as a Drug Exposure: Exposures are individual records corresponding to the source when Drug was delivered to the Person, while successive periods of Drug Exposures are combined under certain rules to produce continuous Drug Eras. Every record in the DRUG_EXPOSURE table should be part of a drug era based on the dates of exposure.

slots.name: drug_era_id
slots.description: NA

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: drug_era_start_date
slots.description: NA

slots.name: drug_era_end_date
slots.description: NA

slots.name: drug_exposure_count
slots.description: The count of grouped DRUG_EXPOSURE records that were included in the DRUG_ERA row

slots.name: gap_days
slots.description: NA

dose_era.name: dose_era
dose_era.description: A Dose Era is defined as a span of time when the Person is assumed to be exposed to a constant dose of a specific active ingredient.

slots.name: dose_era_id
slots.description: NA

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: dose_value
slots.description: The numeric value of the dosage of the drug_ingredient.

slots.name: dose_era_start_date
slots.description: The date the Person started on the specific dosage, with at least 31 days since any prior exposure.

slots.name: dose_era_end_date
slots.description: NA

condition_era.name: condition_era
condition_era.description:
A Condition Era is defined as a span of time when the Person is assumed to have a given condition. Similar to Drug Eras, Condition Eras are chronological periods of Condition Occurrence and every Condition Occurrence record should be part of a Condition Era. Combining individual Condition Occurrences into a single Condition Era serves two purposes:

- It allows aggregation of chronic conditions that require frequent ongoing care, instead of treating each Condition Occurrence as an independent event.
- It allows aggregation of multiple, closely timed doctor visits for the same Condition to avoid double-counting the Condition Occurrences.
For example, consider a Person who visits her Primary Care Physician (PCP) and who is referred to a specialist. At a later time, the Person visits the specialist, who confirms the PCP's original diagnosis and provides the appropriate treatment to resolve the condition. These two independent doctor visits should be aggregated into one Condition Era.

slots.name: condition_era_id
slots.description: NA

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: condition_era_start_date
slots.description:
The start date for the Condition Era
constructed from the individual
instances of Condition Occurrences.
It is the start date of the very first
chronologically recorded instance of
the condition with at least 31 days since any prior record of the same Condition.

slots.name: condition_era_end_date
slots.description:
The end date for the Condition Era
constructed from the individual
instances of Condition Occurrences.
It is the end date of the final
continuously recorded instance of the
Condition.

slots.name: condition_occurrence_count
slots.description:
The number of individual Condition
Occurrences used to construct the
condition era.

episode.name: episode
episode.description: The EPISODE table aggregates lower-level clinical events (VISIT_OCCURRENCE, DRUG_EXPOSURE, PROCEDURE_OCCURRENCE, DEVICE_EXPOSURE) into a higher-level abstraction representing clinically and analytically relevant disease phases,outcomes and treatments. The EPISODE_EVENT table connects qualifying clinical events (VISIT_OCCURRENCE, DRUG_EXPOSURE, PROCEDURE_OCCURRENCE, DEVICE_EXPOSURE) to the appropriate EPISODE entry. For example cancers including their development over time, their treatment, and final resolution.

slots.name: episode_id
slots.description: Use this field to link the EPISODE_EVENT record to its EPISODE.

slots.name: person_id
slots.description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the episode is recorded.

slots.name: episode_start_date
slots.description: The date when the Episode beings.

slots.name: episode_start_datetime
slots.description: The date and time when the Episode begins.

slots.name: episode_end_date
slots.description: The date when the instance of the Episode is considered to have ended.

slots.name: episode_end_datetime
slots.description: The date when the instance of the Episode is considered to have ended.

slots.name: episode_parent_id
slots.description: Use this field to find the Episode that subsumes the given Episode record. This is used in the case that an Episode are nested into each other.

slots.name: episode_number
slots.description: For sequences of episodes, this is used to indicate the order the episodes occurred. For example, lines of treatment could be indicated here.

slots.name: episode_source_value
slots.description: The source code for the Episode as it appears in the source data. This code is mapped to a Standard Condition Concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original code is stored here for reference.

episode_event.name: episode_event
episode_event.description: The EPISODE_EVENT table connects qualifying clinical events (such as CONDITION_OCCURRENCE, DRUG_EXPOSURE, PROCEDURE_OCCURRENCE, MEASUREMENT) to the appropriate EPISODE entry. For example, linking the precise location of the metastasis (cancer modifier in MEASUREMENT) to the disease episode.

slots.name: episode_id
slots.description: Use this field to link the EPISODE_EVENT record to its EPISODE.

slots.name: event_id
slots.description: This field is the primary key of the linked record in the database. For example, if the Episode Event is a Condition Occurrence, then the CONDITION_OCCURRENCE_ID of the linked record goes in this field.

metadata.name: metadata
metadata.description: The METADATA table contains metadata information about a dataset that has been transformed to the OMOP Common Data Model.

slots.name: metadata_id
slots.description: The unique key given to a Metadata record.

slots.name: name
slots.description: NA

slots.name: value_as_string
slots.description: NA

slots.name: value_as_number
slots.description: This is the numerical value of the result of the Metadata, if applicable and available. It is not expected that all Metadata will have numeric results, rather, this field is here to house values should they exist.

slots.name: metadata_date
slots.description: NA

slots.name: metadata_datetime
slots.description: NA

cdm_source.name: cdm_source
cdm_source.description: The CDM_SOURCE table contains detail about the source database and the process used to transform the data into the OMOP Common Data Model.

slots.name: cdm_source_name
slots.description: The name of the CDM instance.

slots.name: cdm_source_abbreviation
slots.description: The abbreviation of the CDM instance.

slots.name: cdm_holder
slots.description: The holder of the CDM instance.

slots.name: source_description
slots.description: The description of the CDM instance.

slots.name: source_documentation_reference
slots.description: Refers to a publication or web resource describing the source data

slots.name: cdm_etl_reference
slots.description: NA

slots.name: source_release_date
slots.description: The date the data was extracted from the source system. In some systems that is the same as the date the ETL was run. Typically the latest even date in the source is on the source_release_date.

slots.name: cdm_release_date
slots.description: The date the ETL script was completed. Typically this is after the source_release_date.

slots.name: cdm_version
slots.description: Version of the OMOP CDM used as string. e.g. v5.4

slots.name: vocabulary_version
slots.description:
Version of the Vocabulary as indicated in
the source.

concept.name: concept
concept.description: The Standardized Vocabularies contains records, or Concepts, that uniquely identify each fundamental unit of meaning used to express clinical information in all domain tables of the CDM. Concepts are derived from vocabularies, which represent clinical information across a domain (e.g. conditions, drugs, procedures) through the use of codes and associated descriptions. Some Concepts are designated Standard Concepts, meaning these Concepts can be used as normative expressions of a clinical entity within the OMOP Common Data Model and standardized analytics. Each Standard Concept belongs to one Domain, which defines the location where the Concept would be expected to occur within the data tables of the CDM. Concepts can represent broad categories ('Cardiovascular disease'), detailed clinical elements ('Myocardial infarction of the anterolateral wall'), or modifying characteristics and attributes that define Concepts at various levels of detail (severity of a disease, associated morphology, etc.). Records in the Standardized Vocabularies tables are derived from national or international vocabularies such as SNOMED-CT, RxNorm, and LOINC, or custom OMOP Concepts defined to cover various aspects of observational data analysis.

slots.name: concept_name
slots.description: An unambiguous, meaningful and descriptive name for the Concept.

slots.name: domain_id
slots.description: A unique key for each domain.

slots.name: vocabulary_id
slots.description:
A unique identifier for each Vocabulary, such
as ICD9CM, SNOMED, Visit.

slots.name: concept_class_id
slots.description: A unique key for each class.

slots.name: standard_concept
slots.description:
This flag determines where a Concept is
a Standard Concept, i.e. is used in the
data, a Classification Concept, or a
non-standard Source Concept. The
allowable values are 'S' (Standard
Concept) and 'C' (Classification
Concept), otherwise the content is NULL.

slots.name: concept_code
slots.description:
The concept code represents the identifier
of the Concept in the source vocabulary,
such as SNOMED-CT concept IDs,
RxNorm RXCUIs etc. Note that concept
codes are not unique across vocabularies.

slots.name: valid_start_date
slots.description:
The date when the Concept was first
recorded. The default value is
1-Jan-1970.

slots.name: valid_end_date
slots.description: The date when then Concept became invalid.

slots.name: invalid_reason
slots.description: Reason the concept was invalidated. Possible values are D (deleted), U (replaced with an update) or NULL when valid_end_date has the default value.

slots.name: concept_id
slots.description: NA

vocabulary.name: vocabulary
vocabulary.description: The VOCABULARY table includes a list of the Vocabularies integrated from various sources or created de novo in OMOP CDM. This reference table contains a single record for each Vocabulary and includes a descriptive name and other associated attributes for the Vocabulary.

slots.name: vocabulary_id
slots.description:
A unique identifier for each Vocabulary, such
as ICD9CM, SNOMED, Visit.

slots.name: vocabulary_name
slots.description:
The name describing the vocabulary, for
example, International Classification of
Diseases, Ninth Revision, Clinical
Modification, Volume 1 and 2 (NCHS) etc.

slots.name: vocabulary_reference
slots.description:
External reference to documentation or
available download of the about the
vocabulary.

slots.name: vocabulary_version
slots.description:
Version of the Vocabulary as indicated in
the source.

domain.name: domain
domain.description: The DOMAIN table includes a list of OMOP-defined Domains to which the Concepts of the Standardized Vocabularies can belong. A Domain represents a clinical definition whereby we assign matching Concepts for the standardized fields in the CDM tables. For example, the Condition Domain contains Concepts that describe a patient condition, and these Concepts can only be used in the condition_concept_id field of the CONDITION_OCCURRENCE and CONDITION_ERA tables. This reference table is populated with a single record for each Domain, including a Domain ID and a descriptive name for every Domain.

slots.name: domain_id
slots.description: A unique key for each domain.

slots.name: domain_name
slots.description:
The name describing the Domain, e.g.
Condition, Procedure, Measurement
etc.

concept_class.name: concept_class
concept_class.description: The CONCEPT_CLASS table includes semantic categories that reference the source structure of each Vocabulary. Concept Classes represent so-called horizontal (e.g. MedDRA, RxNorm) or vertical levels (e.g. SNOMED) of the vocabulary structure. Vocabularies without any Concept Classes, such as HCPCS, use the vocabulary_id as the Concept Class. This reference table is populated with a single record for each Concept Class, which includes a Concept Class ID and a fully specified Concept Class name.

slots.name: concept_class_id
slots.description: A unique key for each class.

slots.name: concept_class_name
slots.description:
The name describing the Concept Class, e.g.
Clinical Finding, Ingredient, etc.

concept_relationship.name: concept_relationship
concept_relationship.description: The CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP table contains records that define relationships between any two Concepts and the nature or type of the relationship. This table captures various types of relationships, including hierarchical, associative, and other semantic connections, enabling comprehensive analysis and interpretation of clinical concepts. Every kind of relationship is defined in the RELATIONSHIP table.

slots.name: relationship_id
slots.description:
The type of relationship captured by the
relationship record.

slots.name: valid_start_date
slots.description:
The date when the Concept was first
recorded. The default value is
1-Jan-1970.

slots.name: valid_end_date
slots.description: The date when then Concept became invalid.

slots.name: invalid_reason
slots.description: Reason the concept was invalidated. Possible values are D (deleted), U (replaced with an update) or NULL when valid_end_date has the default value.

relationship.name: relationship
relationship.description: The RELATIONSHIP table provides a reference list of all types of relationships that can be used to associate any two Concepts in the CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP table, the respective reverse relationships, and their hierarchical characteristics. Note, that Concepts representing relationships between the clinical facts, used for filling in the FACT_RELATIONSHIP table are stored in the CONCEPT table and belong to the Relationship Domain.

slots.name: relationship_id
slots.description:
The type of relationship captured by the
relationship record.

slots.name: relationship_name
slots.description: NA

slots.name: is_hierarchical
slots.description:
Defines whether a relationship defines
concepts into classes or hierarchies. Values
are 1 for hierarchical relationship or 0 if not.

slots.name: defines_ancestry
slots.description:
Defines whether a hierarchical relationship
contributes to the concept_ancestor table.
These are subsets of the hierarchical
relationships. Valid values are 1 or 0.

slots.name: reverse_relationship_id
slots.description:
The identifier for the relationship used to
define the reverse relationship between two
concepts.

concept_synonym.name: concept_synonym
concept_synonym.description: The CONCEPT_SYNONYM table captures alternative terms, synonyms, and translations of Concept Name into various languages linked to specific concepts, providing users with a comprehensive view of how Concepts may be expressed or referenced.

slots.name: concept_synonym_name
slots.description: NA

concept_ancestor.name: concept_ancestor
concept_ancestor.description: The CONCEPT_ANCESTOR table is designed to simplify observational analysis by providing the complete hierarchical relationships between Concepts. Only direct parent-child relationships between Concepts are stored in the CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP table. To determine higher-level ancestry connections, all individual direct relationships would have to be navigated at analysis time. The CONCEPT_ANCESTOR table includes records for all parent-child relationships, as well as grandparent-grandchild relationships and those of any other level of lineage for Standard or Classification concepts. Using the CONCEPT_ANCESTOR table allows for querying for all descendants of a hierarchical concept, and the other way around. For example, drug ingredients and drug products, beneath them in the hierarchy, are all descendants of a drug class ancestor. This table is entirely derived from the CONCEPT, CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP, and RELATIONSHIP tables.

slots.name: min_levels_of_separation
slots.description:
The minimum separation in number of
levels of hierarchy between ancestor and
descendant concepts. This is an attribute
that is used to simplify hierarchic analysis.

slots.name: max_levels_of_separation
slots.description:
The maximum separation in number of
levels of hierarchy between ancestor and
descendant concepts. This is an attribute
that is used to simplify hierarchic analysis.

source_to_concept_map.name: source_to_concept_map
source_to_concept_map.description: The source to concept map table is recommended for use in ETL processes to maintain local source codes which are not available as Concepts in the Standardized Vocabularies, and to establish mappings for each source code into a Standard Concept as target_concept_ids that can be used to populate the Common Data Model tables. The SOURCE_TO_CONCEPT_MAP table is no longer populated with content within the Standardized Vocabularies published to the OMOP community. **There are OHDSI tools to help you populate this table; [Usagi](https://github.com/OHDSI/Usagi) and [Perseus](https://github.com/ohdsi/Perseus). You can read more about OMOP vocabulary mapping in [The Book of OHDSI Chapter 6.3](https://ohdsi.github.io/TheBookOfOhdsi/ExtractTransformLoad.html#step-2-create-the-code-mappings).**

slots.name: source_code
slots.description:
The source code being translated
into a Standard Concept.

slots.name: source_vocabulary_id
slots.description:
A foreign key to the
VOCABULARY table defining the
vocabulary of the source code that
is being translated to a Standard
Concept.

slots.name: source_code_description
slots.description:
An optional description for the
source code. This is included as a
convenience to compare the
description of the source code to
the name of the concept.

slots.name: target_vocabulary_id
slots.description: The Vocabulary of the target Concept.

slots.name: valid_start_date
slots.description:
The date when the Concept was first
recorded. The default value is
1-Jan-1970.

slots.name: valid_end_date
slots.description: The date when then Concept became invalid.

slots.name: invalid_reason
slots.description: Reason the concept was invalidated. Possible values are D (deleted), U (replaced with an update) or NULL when valid_end_date has the default value.

drug_strength.name: drug_strength
drug_strength.description: The DRUG_STRENGTH table contains structured content about the amount or concentration and associated units of a specific ingredient contained within a particular drug product. This table is supplemental information to support standardized analysis of drug utilization.

slots.name: amount_value
slots.description: The numeric value or the amount of active ingredient contained within the drug product.

slots.name: numerator_value
slots.description: The concentration of the active ingredient contained within the drug product.

slots.name: denominator_value
slots.description: The amount of total liquid (or other divisible product, such as ointment, gel, spray, etc.).

slots.name: box_size
slots.description: The number of units of Clinical Branded Drug or Quantified Clinical or Branded Drug contained in a box as dispensed to the patient.

slots.name: valid_start_date
slots.description:
The date when the Concept was first
recorded. The default value is
1-Jan-1970.

slots.name: valid_end_date
slots.description: The date when then Concept became invalid.

slots.name: invalid_reason
slots.description: Reason the concept was invalidated. Possible values are D (deleted), U (replaced with an update) or NULL when valid_end_date has the default value.
